narrative:
First Warn Forecast
Tuesday, May 19, 2026
First Warn Headlines
Flooding concerns continue this morning with widespread showers and thunderstorms producing heavy rainfall.
A few strong to severe thunderstorms remain possible this afternoon into early evening.
The primary severe weather threats will be damaging winds and large hail. Cooler and drier weather arrives Wednesday.

Showers and thunderstorms will continue moving across portions of Missouri and Illinois this morning as a cold front slowly pushes through the region. Some areas have already picked up significant rainfall over the last 24 hours, with many locations receiving 1 to 3 inches of rain and isolated spots seeing even higher totals. Additional heavy rainfall this morning may quickly lead to flash flooding, especially in low-lying and flood-prone areas. Creeks, streams, and poor drainage locations could experience rapid rises in water levels. A Flood Watch remains in effect through this afternoon for portions of the St. Louis metro and surrounding communities.

The severe weather threat today will largely depend on how quickly clouds and morning rain can move out of the area. If enough sunshine develops this afternoon, the atmosphere will become increasingly unstable ahead of the approaching cold front. That would allow scattered thunderstorms to redevelop later today and into the early evening hours.

The greatest potential for severe weather appears to be along and southeast of the I-44 corridor in Missouri and along and south of I-70 in Illinois. Any storm that becomes severe will be capable of producing damaging wind gusts and large hail. A few storms may become briefly intense if the atmosphere can recover enough from this morning’s rainfall activity.

By tonight, the cold front will move southeast of the region, bringing an end to the storm chances. Cooler air will filter into the area overnight, setting the stage for a much cooler Wednesday with temperatures running 10 to 15 degrees below average for mid-May.

Looking ahead, quieter weather is expected Wednesday and Thursday before additional rain chances return late Thursday night into Friday. Temperatures will gradually moderate heading into the upcoming weekend.
